# Chaos-MCP — Mutation-Testing Self-Report

**Generated by the server's own `triage_test_coverage` and `audit_code_resilience` tools**, run against `src/` (22 files, 0 skipped, 0 errored). Each file was sandbox-isolated and subjected to real StrykerJS mutation testing.

## Aggregate

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Files audited | 22 |
| Total mutants generated | 3,729 |
| Killed | 3,296 |
| Survived | 416 |
| No-coverage | 17 |
| **Overall mutation score** | **≈ 88.4%** |
| Files at a perfect 100% | 7 |

## Interpretation

- **`tool-schema.ts` (39%) is an expected false low, not a real gap.** It's the static MCP tool-definition object — almost entirely `description` string literals. Stryker mutates every string, but those mutations are **equivalent mutants**: changing schema prose changes no observable behavior any test could assert against. This is exactly the "don't chase equivalent mutants" case CLAUDE.md and the self-audit memory call out. Asserting on every description string would be brittle busywork.

- **The real, addressable concentration is `handler.ts` (106 survivors) and `config-loader.ts` (49 survivors).** These are the orchestrator and the precedence-resolution logic — genuine behavioral code where survivors most likely point at untested branches (validator ordering, args > engine-section > global precedence, scope/short-circuit edges). Highest payoff for hardening.

- **Engine adapters are solid**: TS 85.9%, Python 88.4%, Rust 94%, Go 94% — and the four pure-logic utils (`git-diff`, `exec-classify`, `logger`, plus `base`/`registry`) are at a clean 100%.

- **17 no-coverage mutants** (clustered in `config-loader`, `sandbox`, `typescript`) are code paths *no test executes at all* — usually a faster win than killing a survivor, since they just need a test to touch the line.

---

## Drill-down: `src/handler.ts` (84.41%, 106 survivors, worst severity: high)

After excluding the ~30 low-severity `StringLiteral` survivors in log lines and error
messages (equivalent / cosmetic — not worth chasing), the real gaps cluster into 7 themes:

| # | Theme | Lines | What to add |
|--:|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Validator predicates** — each `\|\|`/`&&` arm forced constant; one field's reject-arm untested | 142, 158, 160-161, 194, 210-216 | One test per field with *that* field wrong-typed (e.g. `lineScope.start` valid but `.end` a string; baseline entry with bad `line` vs bad `mutators`) |
| 2 | **Boundary off-by-one** — `entry.line < 1`, `relFromRoot.length > 0` | 214, 655 | Assert at exactly the boundary: `line: 0` rejected / `line: 1` accepted |
| 3 | **Type-guard helpers** `isValidConcurrency` (1..64), `isPositiveMs`, `isValidLineScope` | 253, 255-256, 266, 271 | Boundary cases: concurrency `0/1/64/65`, ms `0/1` |
| 4 | **`buildRunOptions` precedence** | 312, 320, 335, 343 | mutmut-vs-stryker testRunner branch; `timeoutMs > 0` fallback; outputFormat `json`; `ignorePatterns` filtering out non-strings |
| 5 | **Prebuild time-budget** `remaining > 0 ? remaining : 1` | 438, 440 | Force a prebuild that nearly exhausts `timeoutMs`; assert the deducted remainder (boundary at 0) |
| 6 | **Diff/baseline scope routing** | 484, 515, 537-538, 569 | "no changes" + verify paths under both `outputFormat: text` and `json`; baseline object-guard |
| 7 | **Source-read failure** `catch { sourceLines = undefined }` | 389 | Make the file unreadable in-sandbox; assert enrich degrades gracefully |

The `isVerbose()`-gated logging survivors (421, 436, 703, 709) are low value — diagnostics only.
The one worth a test is the `env.packageManager !== 'pip'` label ternary (425).

## Drill-down: `src/utils/config-loader.ts` (89.31%, 49 survivors + 1 no-coverage line)

Nearly every survivor is the same shape — **a type-check accept-arm never exercised with a bad value**:

| # | Theme | Lines | What to add |
|--:|---|---|---|
| 1 | **`parse*Config` accept-arms** — `typeof x === 'number' → true` survives | 151, 156, 177, 198, 203, 221, 226, 266, 280, 284, 294, 311 | For each field, pass a *wrong-typed* value (e.g. `timeoutMs: "5"`) and assert it is **dropped**, not applied. The `=== null` guards (151/198/221/411) need a `null` input asserted to return `undefined` |
| 2 | **`validateConfig` warnings** | 374, 379, 381, 385, 388, 391 | Assert the *specific* warning string fires per wrong type; concurrency boundary `<1` / `>64` |
| 3 | **`validateEngineSection` per-key** | 433, 444, 450, 457-458, 466, 474 | Per-key wrong-type warning; concurrency boundary `1`/`64` at 450 |
| 4 | **`validFieldCount++`** (UpdateOperator) | 481 | Assert the post-loop field count / that a section with N valid fields is retained |
| 5 | **No-coverage: `allowPrebuild` non-boolean** — *no test reaches this branch at all* | 391-392 | Add a config with `allowPrebuild: "yes"`; assert the boolean warning is produced |

The 4 `StringLiteral` survivors (lines 9, 15, 263, 426) are key-name / `'utf-8'` / `', '` literals — equivalent mutants, skip.

## Highest-leverage takeaways

1. **The dominant pattern across both files is "valid-input tested, invalid-input not."** A batch of
   negative tests — each passing one wrong-typed field and asserting it's rejected/dropped — would
   kill the large majority of high-severity survivors in one sweep.
2. **`config-loader.ts:391` is the one true blind spot** (no-coverage): the `allowPrebuild`
   boolean-validation warning has zero test reaching it. Fastest single win.
3. **Boundary tests** (concurrency 1/64, line ≥1, remaining-ms >0) close the `EqualityOperator`
   survivors that pure type-tests won't.

---

## Results after hardening (tests added)

26 targeted tests were added across `handler-helpers.test.ts` (+12) and `config-loader.test.ts`
(+14), then re-verified by re-running the mutation audit. Full gate (`npm run check`) green:
**1001 passed | 6 skipped**.

| File | Before | After | Survivors |
|---|--:|--:|---|
| `src/utils/config-loader.ts` | 89.31% | **98.02%** | 49 → **10** |
| `src/handler.ts` | 84.41% | **90.74%** | 106 → **63** |

`handler.ts` was hardened in two passes: a pure-function batch (84.41% → 88.24%) and then an
`isPrebuildAllowed` + `handleToolCall` integration batch (88.24% → **90.74%**) covering the
security gate and A2/A3 routing — see "Second hardening pass" below.

What the new tests killed (verified, not assumed):
- **config-loader:** the `allowPrebuild` no-coverage branch; every `parse*Config` `=== null`
  guard (null-section no-crash); the coercible-string `timeoutMs` typeof checks; the float-concurrency
  `&&`→`||` mutant; the bare-number / null top-level config message; per-key `validateEngineSection`
  dispatch (`perMutantTimeoutMs`, `mutatorDenylist`, valid-array/boolean/string no-warn); the
  denylist-only `hasAny`; the concurrency-65 upper bound.
- **handler:** baseline error-message specificity (null / non-object / null-entry / null-mutators /
  string-mutators); the `line < 1` boundary; the mutmut-runner leak guard for non-mutmut engines;
  wrong-typed `timeoutMs` / `perMutantTimeoutMs` fallbacks; `outputFormat: 'json'`; `ignorePatterns`
  filtering; the `isValidConcurrency` lower bound.

### Why the remainder is left (equivalent or integration-path)

`config-loader.ts` is effectively maxed — **all 10 remaining survivors are provably equivalent
mutants**: `typeof x === 'number' → true` guarded by a downstream `Number.isInteger` (lines 161, 284,
294, 379, 385), the `val < 1` arm at 450 made unreachable by the earlier `val <= 0` guard, the
`validFieldCount++ → --` sign flip that can't change a `=== 0` outcome (481), and three cosmetic
`StringLiteral`s (key name, `'utf-8'`, `', '`). None are killable without asserting on
behaviorally-identical output.

`handler.ts`'s remaining survivors are dominated by (a) `StringLiteral` mutations in log lines and
error-message prose (cosmetic / equivalent), (b) `isVerbose()`-gated diagnostic logging, and
(c) a handful of prebuild-timing edge branches — see the next section.

---

## Second hardening pass — `isPrebuildAllowed` + `handleToolCall` integration (88.24% → 90.74%)

A focused batch added 11 tests (5 in `handler-helpers.test.ts`, 6 in `handler.test.ts`), each
re-verified by re-running the audit. Full gate green: **1012 passed | 6 skipped**.

Killed (verified):
- **`isPrebuildAllowed` env-var gate (line 64)** — the security opt-in that lets the
  `CHAOS_MCP_ALLOW_PREBUILD` env var enable arbitrary-shell prebuilds. Only the *config* branch was
  tested before; the env branch (`'1'` / `'true'` enable, everything else rejects) was a blind spot.
  Exported the function and unit-tested every token.
- **A2 no-change short-circuit** — the synthetic `100.00%` / empty-`vulnerabilities` result returned
  when a diff has no changes, in both JSON and text form (lines 510/511/515).
- **A3 verify-mode routing** — text-vs-JSON delta formatting when a `baseline` is supplied (line 569).
- **Prebuild error fidelity (line 749)** — a prebuild failure is returned verbatim, not re-wrapped by
  the generic `Chaos Engine Halted:` outer catch.
- **Ignored-options note (line 91)** — only the StrykerJS-only options actually passed are listed for
  a non-TS engine, not all seven.

### Why the final 63 are left

Re-reading the source confirmed several candidates are in fact **equivalent**, not killable:
- **389** `catch { sourceLines = undefined }` — `sourceLines` is already `undefined` from its
  declaration, so emptying the catch yields an identical value.
- **418 / 484 / 537 / 538** — all guarded by `validateToolArgs` running first (diffBase/baseline are
  already string-or-undefined / well-formed-object-or-undefined by the time these run).
- **655** `relFromRoot.length > 0` — `relative(root, file)` is only `''` when the two are equal, which
  can't happen for a file inside its workspace root.

The genuine remainder is: `StringLiteral` log/error prose (~35, cosmetic), `isVerbose()`-gated
diagnostics (421/436/703/709), the validator typeof/`Number.isInteger` and `normalizeLineScope`
defensive redundancy (142/158/160/161/211/253/255/256/266 — equivalent), `OptionalChaining` on
guaranteed-present registry/argument lookups (90/301/367/614 — equivalent), the `pairCount` sign flip
(221 — equivalent), and a few low-value prebuild-timing edges (425/438/440/720/722/754). All are
either equivalent or cosmetic; the behavior-bearing logic is now covered. The recommended close-out is
a `mutatorDenylist: ["StringLiteral"]` in the self-audit Stryker config so future runs report a clean
signal instead of re-flagging prose.
